gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (1/365)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (1/365)
text: خَرَجْنَا بِسَمَكٍ نَتَلَقَّى بِهِ أَبَا الْحَسَنِ الرِّضَا ( عليه السلام ) وَ قَدْ خَرَجْنَا مِنَ الْمَدِينَةِ وَ قَدْ قَدِمَ هُوَ مِنْ سَفَرٍ لَهُ فَقَالَ وَيْحَكَ يَا فُلَانُ لَعَلَّ مَعَكَ سَمَكاً فَقُلْتُ نَعَمْ يَا سَيِّدِي جُعِلْتُ فِدَاكَ فَقَالَ انْزِلُوا ثُمَّ قَالَ وَيْحَكَ لَعَلَّهُ زَهْوٌ قَالَ قُلْتُ نَعَمْ فَأَرَيْتُهُ فَقَالَ ارْكَبُوا لَا حَاجَةَ لَنَا فِيهِ وَ الزَّهْوُ سَمَكٌ لَيْسَ لَهُ قِشْرٌ .

en.hubeali: Abu Ali Al Ashary, from Al Hassan Bin Ali, from his uncle Muhammad, from Suleyman Bin Ja’far, from Is’haq, owner of the fish, who said,

‘We went out with fish to meet Abu Al-Hassan Al-Reza<sup>asws</sup> with it, and we had exited from Al-Medina, and he<sup>asws</sup> had proceeded from a journey of his<sup>asws</sup>. So he<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘Woe be unto you, O so and so! Perhaps there are fishes with you?’ So I said, ‘Yes, O my master<sup>asws</sup>, may I be sacrificed for you<sup>asws</sup>!’ So he<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘Descend you all!’ Then he<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘Woe be unto you, perhaps it is Siganus?’ I said, ‘Yes’. So I showed it to him<sup>asws</sup>. So he<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘Ride! There is no need for us<sup>asws</sup> with regards to it, and the Siganus is a fish which has no scales on it’.
en.sarwar: 10. Abu Ali al-Ash‘ariy has narrated from al-Hassan ibn Ali from his uncle Muhammad ibn Sulayman ibn Ja‘far who has said the following: “Ishaq, fisherman, narrated to me the following, ‘Once we went out with fish to meet abu al-Hassan, al-Rida’, ‘Alayhi al-Salam. We went out of al-Madinah and he (the Imam) was coming from his journey and he (the Imam) said, ‘Fie upon, you O so and so, perhaps you have fish with you.’ I replied, ‘Yes, O my master, I pray to Allah to keep my soul in service for your cause.’ He (the Imam) said, ‘Bring them down.’ He (the Imam) then said, ‘Perhaps it is Zahw.’ I replied, ‘Yes, it is and I showed him (the Imam) Zahw ’ He (the Imam) said, ‘Ride your stumpers; we cannot use them for food.’ Al- Zahw is a fish that has no scales.’”
